major_characters.description: Former Avenger turned vigilante after his family's loss; joins the heist and retrieves the Soul Stone.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Avenge and cope with his family's death by helping reverse the snap and honor Natasha's sacrifice.

major_characters.name: Bruce Banner / Smart Hulk
major_characters.description: Scientist who merges Hulk strength with Banner intellect and performs the restorative snap.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Use his unique physiology to wield the Stones' gamma radiation and bring everyone back.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary:
Princess Leia Organa obtains stolen schematics for the Empire's Death Star and hides them in droid R2-D2, who escapes with C-3PO in an escape pod as her Star Destroyer is boarded by Darth Vader. The pod crashes on Tatooine; Jawas capture the droids and sell them to moisture farmers Owen and Beru Lars, whose nephew Luke Skywalker retrieves R2-D2 and discovers Leia's holographic plea to Obi-Wan Kenobi. Luke seeks out Ben (Obi-Wan) Kenobi, who reveals he was a Jedi and owned Luke's father; he gives Luke his father's lightsaber and offers to take him to Alderaan to help the Rebellion. Imperial stormtroopers arrive on Tatooine searching for the droids; they kill Owen and Beru when they cannot find Luke and Obi-Wan, prompting Luke to join Obi-Wan.

In Mos Eisley, Obi-Wan and Luke hire smuggler Han Solo and his co-pilot Chewbacca aboard the Millennium Falcon to carry them to Alderaan. Han accepts payment up front and later kills bounty hunter Greedo when threatened. En route, the Falcon is captured by the Death Star's tractor beam; the group hides aboard and infiltrates the station. R2-D2 locates Leia, who is a prisoner scheduled for execution. Luke, Han, and Chewie rescue Leia from her cell; Obi-Wan disables the tractor beam but confronts Darth Vader and is struck down, vanishing while distracting Vader so the others can escape.

The Death Star demonstrates its power by destroying Alderaan under Grand Moff Tarkin's orders; Tarkin then orders a search for the rebel base. Back with the Rebel Alliance at Yavin IV, the stolen schematics in R2-D2 are analyzed and reveal a two-meter exhaust port that, if hit precisely, will trigger a chain reaction to destroy the Death Star. The Rebels scramble starfighters for an attack before the station can fire on the Yavin base. Luke joins Red Squadron in an X-wing; Han initially declines but returns in the Millennium Falcon at the critical moment. Darth Vader leads Imperial TIE fighters against the assault and almost destroys Luke. Guided by Obi-Wan's voice and using the Force, Luke disables his targeting computer and fires proton torpedoes into the exhaust port, igniting the reactor chain and causing the Death Star to explode. Han, Luke, and Leia receive medals at a victory ceremony aboard the Rebel base.
original_full_tokens.setting: Far-future galaxy, planets including Tatooine, Alderaan, Death Star, Yavin IV

major_characters.name: Luke Skywalker
major_characters.description: Tatooine moisture farmer's nephew turned Rebel pilot and would-be Jedi.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Leave Tatooine, learn the ways of the Force, and help the Rebellion after his family is killed.

major_characters.name: Princess Leia Organa
major_characters.description: Rebel leader and Alderaanian senator who hides Death Star plans in R2-D2.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Protect the Rebellion by delivering the Death Star schematics and find safety for the Alliance.

major_characters.name: Obi-Wan "Ben" Kenobi
major_characters.description: Aging former Jedi who mentors Luke and sacrifices himself confronting Darth Vader.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Protect the Death Star plans, reconnect Luke with the Jedi legacy, and enable the Rebels' escape.

major_characters.name: Han Solo
major_characters.description: Smuggler and captain of the Millennium Falcon, initially motivated by profit.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Get paid to transport passengers and escape Jabba's debt, later helps Rebels and returns at the decisive moment.

major_characters.name: Darth Vader
major_characters.description: Imperial Sith lord enforcing the Emperor's will and hunting the Rebels.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Locate the Death Star plans, crush the Rebel Alliance, and enforce Imperial dominance.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary: After Lord Voldemort's attack leaves infant Harry Potter (Daniel Radcliffe) orphaned and marked with a lightning-bolt scar, Albus Dumbledore (Richard Harris), Professor McGonagall (Maggie Smith) and Hagrid (Robbie Coltrane) leave the boy on the doorstep of his Muggle relatives, the Dursleys, who hide his past and mistreat him. On his eleventh birthday Hagrid arrives at a hut on the sea-locked island where the Dursleys have fled, delivers Harry's Hogwarts acceptance letter and brings him into the wizarding world. In Diagon Alley Harry learns he is famous, buys school supplies, and discovers his parents were wizards killed by Voldemort. On the Hogwarts Express he befriends Ron Weasley (Rupert Grint) and meets Hermione Granger (Emma Watson); the Sorting Hat places all three in Gryffindor while Draco Malfoy is sorted into Slytherin. At Hogwarts Harry shows skill as a Seeker and joins the Gryffindor Quidditch team. The trio discover a three-headed dog, Fluffy, guarding a trapdoor and later learn from Hagrid that Nicolas Flamel is associated with a mysterious magical object, the Sorcerer’s (Philosopher’s) Stone, which produces the Elixir of Life. Strange incidents follow: a troll in the castle (which Harry and Ron fight to save Hermione), Harry's broom is jinxed during a Quidditch match, and Harry sees what he believes are suspicious interactions between Professor Severus Snape (Alan Rickman) and Professor Quirrell (Ian Hart). Harry receives an invisibility cloak that once belonged to his father and uses it to research Flamel in the restricted section with Hermione and Ron. In the Forbidden Forest Harry witnesses a hooded figure drinking unicorn blood; the centaur Firenze tells him the figure is Voldemort. Concluding that someone is trying to steal the Stone to return Voldemort to power, Harry, Ron and Hermione break into the guarded chamber: they bypass a series of magical protections (Devil's Snare, flying keys, a life-sized wizard's chess game) together until Harry goes alone into the final chamber. There Harry confronts Professor Quirrell and finds Voldemort living as a weak, parasitic presence on the back of Quirrell's head. Voldemort/Quirrell attempts to seize the Stone, but when Quirrell touches Harry he burns and collapses because Harry carries his mother Lily's sacrificial protection; Voldemort flees, abandoning Quirrell, who dies. Harry loses consciousness and wakes in the hospital wing where Dumbledore explains the protection and reveals that Nicolas Flamel and the Stone will be destroyed to prevent misuse. At the end-of-year feast Dumbledore awards last-minute house points to Harry, Ron and Hermione, delivering the House Cup to Gryffindor.
original_full_tokens.setting: 1990s-era Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry, England

major_characters.name: Harry Potter
major_characters.description: An orphaned eleven-year-old with a lightning-bolt scar who discovers he is a wizard.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Learn who he is and protect Hogwarts from the return of Voldemort.

major_characters.name: Ron Weasley
major_characters.description: A red-haired fellow first-year from a large wizarding family and Harry's friend.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Support Harry, prove his own worth, and help stop the Stone's theft.

major_characters.name: Hermione Granger
major_characters.description: A Muggle-born, exceptionally studious first-year who becomes Harry's friend and strategist.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Use her knowledge and resourcefulness to help protect Hogwarts and her friends.

major_characters.name: Lord Voldemort
major_characters.description: Dark wizard who killed Harry's parents; reduced to a weak, parasitic form seeking restoration.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Regain physical form and immortality by acquiring the Sorcerer’s Stone.

major_characters.name: Professor Quirrell
major_characters.description: Nervous Defense Against the Dark Arts teacher secretly hosting Voldemort.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Steal the Stone for Voldemort and enable Voldemort's return.

major_characters.name: Rubeus Hagrid
major_characters.description: Hogwarts gamekeeper who introduces Harry to the wizarding world and cares for magical creatures.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Protect Harry and support him entering Hogwarts, often through reckless affection for creatures.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary: Prologue: In Middle-earth, the Dark Lord Sauron forges the One Ring to dominate bearers of the Rings of Power. A Last Alliance of Elves and Men defeats Sauron; Isildur cuts the Ring from Sauron's hand but keeps it instead of destroying it. Isildur is later killed and the Ring is lost in a river. Centuries later Gollum possesses the Ring for centuries before Bilbo Baggins finds it in the Goblin tunnels and takes it to the Shire. Sixty years later Bilbo leaves the Ring to his nephew Frodo Baggins at his 111th birthday. Gandalf investigates, learns the artifact is Sauron's One Ring, and discovers that Orcs tortured Gollum who revealed "Shire" and "Baggins." Gandalf warns Frodo to flee the Shire. Frodo departs with Samwise Gamgee to Bree; Merry Brandybuck and Peregrin Took join them. Gandalf rides to Isengard to question Saruman; Saruman has betrayed the White Council, allied with Sauron, and imprisons Gandalf atop Orthanc. In Bree they meet Aragorn (Strider), who escorts them toward Rivendell. On Weathertop the Nazgûl attack; Frodo is stabbed with a Morgul blade and begins to fade. Aragorn leads them toward Rivendell while Frodo is pursued by the Ringwraiths. The elf Arwen intercepts and brings Frodo into Rivendell, where Elrond heals him. At the Council of Elrond representatives of Elves, Dwarves, and Men debate the Ring's fate; only Mount Doom's fires will destroy it. Frodo volunteers to carry the Ring to Mordor. A Fellowship is formed to help him: Frodo, Sam, Merry, Pippin, Gandalf, Aragorn, Legolas, Gimli, and Boromir. They attempt to cross the Redhorn Pass at Caradhras, but are turned back by snow and Saruman's malice. The Fellowship enters the Mines of Moria to cross under the mountain. Pippin alerts orcs, and while fleeing they encounter Durin's Bane, a Balrog, on the Bridge of Khazad-dûm. Gandalf confronts the Balrog, breaks the bridge, and falls with the demon into the abyss. The Fellowship flees to Lothlórien, where Galadriel and Celeborn shelter them; Galadriel gives Frodo gifts, warns him that one of the Fellowship will try to take the Ring, and shows him a dark vision. They travel down the Anduin by boats; at Parth Galen Boromir tries to take the Ring from Frodo. Frodo puts the Ring on and flees; realizing the Fellowship is compromised, Frodo decides to continue alone. Merry and Pippin distract an attacking force of Uruk-hai to let Frodo escape; Boromir is mortally wounded defending them and confesses his failure to Aragorn before dying. Aragorn, Legolas, and Gimli ride off to rescue the captured Merry and Pippin; Frodo, accompanied only by Sam after Frodo allows him to come, departs for Mordor alone by crossing Emyn Muil and heading toward the black land. The film ends with the Fellowship broken: Frodo and Sam marching toward Mordor, Aragorn leading a pursuit, and Merry and Pippin taken by enemies.
original_full_tokens.setting: Third Age Middle-earth: Shire to Rivendell, Moria, Lothlórien, Anduin

major_characters.name: Frodo Baggins
major_characters.description: A young hobbit and Ring-bearer tasked with destroying the One Ring.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Carry the One Ring to Mount Doom to destroy it and prevent Sauron's return.

major_characters.name: Gandalf
major_characters.description: A wizard of the Istari who discovers the Ring's nature and guides the mission.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Defeat Sauron by ensuring the Ring is destroyed and protect Middle-earth.

major_characters.name: Aragorn (Strider)
major_characters.description: Ranger of the North, heir of Isildur and rightful heir to Gondor's throne.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Protect the Fellowship and reclaim his heritage to help defeat Sauron.

major_characters.name: Samwise Gamgee
major_characters.description: Frodo's gardener and steadfast friend who accompanies him as companion.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Keep Frodo safe and help him complete the quest out of loyalty and love.

major_characters.name: Boromir
major_characters.description: Son of Gondor's Steward, a proud warrior who joins the Fellowship.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Protect Gondor and claim the Ring's power to defend his people, but is corrupted by temptation.

major_characters.name: Legolas
major_characters.description: An Elven prince and skilled archer representing the Elves on the quest.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Aid in the quest to stop Sauron and protect Middle-earth's peoples.

major_characters.name: Gimli
major_characters.description: A Dwarf warrior representing his people on the Fellowship.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Fulfill his oath to aid the Ring-bearer and prove his people's honour.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary:
In 180 A.D., General Maximus Decimus Meridius leads Roman legions to victory against Germanic tribes and is hailed by soldiers and the aging Emperor Marcus Aurelius. Aurelius intends to name Maximus his successor and restore power to the Senate, but his jealous son Commodus murders Aurelius after overhearing the plan. Commodus orders Maximus's execution; Maximus escapes the executioners only to return home and find his villa burned and his wife and son crucified. Captured by slave traders, Maximus is sold in Zucchabar to Proximo, a retired gladiator-owner. Grief-stricken and initially refusing to fight, Maximus kills in the arena when attacked and proves a formidable gladiator, gaining popularity. He befriends fellow gladiators Juba (a Numidian) and Hagen (a Germanic slave).

Commodus reopens the games in Rome to consolidate power. Proximo's troupe is hired to fight in the Colosseum; Maximus leads them to victory in a staged battle recreation and is recognized by the crowd. Commodus is enraged but cannot kill Maximus because of public support. Commodus then pits Maximus against Tigris of Gaul; Maximus defeats Tigris and spares him, earning the epithet "Maximus the Merciful" and increasing his popularity while Commodus's prestige declines. Maximus's former servant Cicero informs him that Maximus's loyal legions are encamped at Portus/Ostia. Lucilla, Commodus's sister, and Senator Gracchus conspire with Maximus to use the army to overthrow Commodus and restore the Republic.

Commodus learns of the conspiracy after torturing Lucilla's son and betrays her. The Praetorian Guard storms Proximo's ludus; Proximo and Hagen are killed, other gladiators are captured, and Maximus narrowly escapes but is pursued. Maximus reaches the city walls and attempts to rally support but is ambushed; Lucilla's plot collapses. Commodus, seeking a public resolution, challenges Maximus to single combat in the Colosseum. Before the duel, Commodus stabs Maximus with a hidden dagger, puncturing his lung and leaving the wound concealed beneath armor. In the public duel Commodus fights dishonorably; Maximus overpowers him, disarms him, and then kills Commodus with the same hidden stiletto after Commodus tries to renew the attack. Mortally wounded from Commodus's earlier stab, Maximus walks to the emperor's box and, with his remaining breath, orders the release of Proximo's surviving gladiators and instructs Senator Gracchus to restore power to the Senate. Maximus dies in the arena; Lucilla is reassured that her son is safe. Juba buries Maximus's small statues of his wife and son in the Colosseum, and Proximo's freed gladiators carry Maximus's body away as Rome moves toward the promised political change.
original_full_tokens.setting: Rome and North Africa, 180 A.D. (Roman Empire)

major_characters.name: Maximus Decimus Meridius
major_characters.description: A respected Roman general turned slave and gladiator.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Avenge the murder of his family and restore Rome's virtue by confronting Commodus.

major_characters.name: Commodus
major_characters.description: Ambitious son of Marcus Aurelius who seizes the throne.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Maintain power as emperor and crush threats to his rule out of jealousy and insecurity.

major_characters.name: Marcus Aurelius

original_full_tokens.plot_summary:
Dominick "Dom" Cobb, an extractor who uses military dream-sharing technology to steal secrets from people's subconscious, and his partner Arthur try to extract from Japanese businessman Saito aboard a business meeting in Tokyo; the extraction is sabotaged by appearances of Cobb's deceased wife, Mal. Saito reveals he was testing them and offers Cobb a job: perform inception—plant an idea in Robert Michael Fischer Jr.'s mind so Fischer will dissolve his father's energy conglomerate. In return Saito will use his influence to clear Cobb's murder charges so Cobb can return to the United States and see his children. Cobb assembles a team in Los Angeles: Arthur (planner), Eames (forger/conman), Yusuf (chemist) who creates a heavy sedative for layered dreaming, and Ariadne (architecture student) to design the dream landscapes; Saito insists on joining to witness success.

The team isolates Fischer on a flight from Sydney to Los Angeles after his father's death, sedates him and themselves, and begin a three-level dream-within-dream inception. Level 1 (Yusuf's rainy downtown) the team abducts Fischer; projections from Fischer's trained subconscious attack and wound Saito. Eames seduces Fischer's perception by impersonating his godfather Peter Browning to plant doubt about his father's intentions. Level 2 (Arthur's hotel) the team manipulates Fischer into believing the kidnapping was staged to push him to make his own choices. Level 3 (Eames's snowy mountain fortress) they prepare the final suggestion that Fischer should break up his father's company and be his "own man." Because the team uses layered dreams, each deeper level requires synchronized "kicks"—forces to wake sleepers—timed by the team members remaining behind in higher levels.

Yusuf, under attack in level 1, tries to kick early by driving the van off a bridge; the timing error removes gravity in the hotel level, causing an avalanche in the mountain level and derailing several kicks. Saito's wound worsens. Cobb's subconscious projection of Mal manifests repeatedly and increasingly sabotages the mission: she kills Fischer in the mountain fortress, sending both Fischer and Saito into limbo (profound, unconstructed subconscious space) because of the heavy sedation—death in the dream can consign sleepers to limbo. Cobb and Ariadne descend into limbo to retrieve Fischer and Saito while Eames stays to arrange the fortress kick with explosives and Arthur improvises a synchronized elevator kick.

In limbo Cobb reveals to Ariadne that he and the real Mal were trapped in limbo decades earlier and spent what felt like decades building an elaborate shared world; when they escaped to waking life, Mal believed they were still dreaming and committed suicide to force them awake, framing Cobb and creating his murder charges. Cobb had become a fugitive, separated from his children. In limbo Cobb chooses to find and revive Saito; Ariadne locates an unconstructed Fischer and pushes him off a balcony to force a revival in the mountain fortress where he finds the planted idea and accepts it. The team executes the remaining kicks: Eames detonates explosives in the fortress, Arthur blasts the elevator in level 2, Yusuf's van hits the water in level 1. Cobb finds an aged Saito in limbo; they remind each other of their bargain, shoot themselves to wake, and all awaken on the airplane.

Back in reality at Los Angeles airport, Saito uses his influence to clear Cobb's record; Cobb passes through U.S. immigration and reunites with his father-in-law Stephen Miles, then sees his children. Cobb tests reality with his totem, a spinning top that indicates dreaming if it spins indefinitely, but he sets it spinning on a table and walks away to embrace his children without watching to see if it falls, leaving the film's final state—whether Cobb is awake or still dreaming—ambiguous.
original_full_tokens.setting: Contemporary international (Tokyo, Sydney, Los Angeles; layered dreamscapes)

original_full_tokens.plot_summary:
In 1999, Thomas A. Anderson (Keanu Reeves), a Bay-area computer programmer who secretly hacks under the alias "Neo," notices cryptic references to "the Matrix" on his computer. After receiving messages — "wake up, Neo" and "follow the white rabbit" — Neo meets Trinity (Carrie-Anne Moss) at a nightclub; Trinity later helps him escape a police/agent raid and introduces him to Morpheus (Laurence Fishburne). Morpheus contacts Neo by phone during a Metacortex office raid by three men in black suits — Agent Smith (Hugo Weaving), Agent Brown and Agent Jones — who attempt to arrest Neo. Morpheus remotely guides Neo's escape but Neo is captured, interrogated and implanted with a tracking probe by the agents. Morpheus's team rescues Neo, removes the probe, and offers him a choice: take the blue pill to forget or the red pill to learn the truth. Neo swallows the red pill and wakes up physically years later, naked and hairless in a pod in a machine farm. He is rescued by the hovercraft Nebuchadnezzar and its crew — Morpheus, Trinity, Tank, Dozer, Apoc, Switch and Mouse — and brought to their Zion-born operators.

Aboard the Nebuchadnezzar, Morpheus explains the truth: the world Neo knew is the Matrix, a neural-interactive simulated reality created by intelligent machines that harvest human bioelectricity; the real world is a devastated, machine-dominated wasteland and the remaining free humans live underground in the city Zion. Morpheus believes Neo is "the One," a prophesied human able to break the Matrix. Tank trains Neo in combat via jacked-in construct programs and Neo learns martial arts and to bend the simulation's rules. Morpheus tests Neo in a rooftop jump program; Neo fails to make a leap. The ship detects machine sentinels and hides in sewers to avoid them. Meanwhile, crew member Cypher (Joe Pantoliano) secretly meets Agent Smith and agrees to betray Morpheus in exchange for being reinserted into the Matrix with erased memories.

Morpheus takes Neo and the crew to see the Oracle (Gloria Foster) inside the Matrix; the Oracle cryptically suggests Neo may not be the One and warns that he will soon face a choice involving Morpheus's life. On the way out, the team is ambushed by police and agents — Mouse is killed, others scatter — and Morpheus allows himself to be captured to enable the others' escape because Cypher has sabotaged their extraction. Back on the Nebuchadnezzar, Cypher unplugs and murders crew members as he betrays them; Tank kills Cypher and extracts Neo, Trinity and a few survivors. The agents interrogate Morpheus in the Matrix, trying to extract Zion's mainframe access codes; Agent Smith explains how previous Matrix iterations failed because humans rejected a perfect world.

Believing Morpheus's capture must be rescued, Neo chooses to return into the Matrix despite the Oracle's ambiguous prophecy. Trinity and Neo re-enter, use a telephone exit plan and infiltrate the building where Morpheus is held. They fight through SWAT and agents; Trinity climbs the elevator shaft to free Morpheus. Neo confronts Agent Smith in the hallway, where Neo increasingly manipulates the Matrix to dodge bullets and fight. The Nebuchadnezzar, meanwhile, is attacked in the real world by swarming sentinels; Tank fires an EMP to disable them but only after the ship is critical. During the rescue, Neo is shot multiple times by Agent Smith and dies in the real world. Trinity, kneeling beside Neo, tells him the Oracle said she would fall in love with the One, kisses him, and in the Matrix her kiss restores him. Neo's understanding and power surge: he now perceives and overrides the Matrix's rules, reverses his death, destroys Agent Smith by destabilizing him across connected bodies, and returns to the real world. Tank fires the EMP, disabling the sentinels and saving the ship.

The film ends with Neo back inside the Matrix making a phone call on a public line: he promises to show the enslaved humans the truth and that he will fight the system; he then takes off, flying upward and away, demonstrating his new ability as "the One."
original_full_tokens.setting: Late 20th-century simulated Matrix; post-apocalyptic real world 2199

major_characters.primary_motivations: Discover the truth about reality and fulfill (or reject) the prophecy of "the One" to free humanity.

major_characters.primary_motivations: Find and train the One to end the Matrix and liberate humanity from the machines.

major_characters.primary_motivations: Maintain the integrity of the Matrix by capturing or eliminating threats like Morpheus and Neo.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary:
In a near-future agrarian United States, former NASA pilot Joseph "Cooper" (Cooper) runs a farm in the American Midwest with his daughter Murphy "Murph" and son Tom as global crop blight and dust storms steadily make Earth uninhabitable. Murph's bedroom appears haunted by a force that encodes binary coordinates in dust; Cooper and Murph follow them to a secret NASA installation led by Professor John Brand. Brand reveals a wormhole near Saturn that leads to another galaxy and three potentially habitable worlds — Miller, Edmunds, and Mann — surveyed by earlier "Lazarus" missions. Brand recruits Cooper to pilot the starship Endurance with scientists Amelia Brand (his daughter), Romilly, Doyle, and two robots, TARS and CASE, to retrieve survey data; if one planet is viable, humanity will emigrate on giant space stations. Brand also has a covert "Plan B": Endurance carries thousands of frozen human embryos to repopulate a new world if Plan A (solving gravity to lift Earth's population) fails.

Cooper leaves Earth after a tearful, estranged parting with Murph. The crew enters the wormhole and first visits Miller's ocean world, which orbits very close to the black hole Gargantua and suffers extreme time dilation (one hour equals seven years on Earth). A landing party is swamped by massive tidal waves; Doyle dies and the mission is delayed. When they return to Endurance, 23 years have elapsed: Murph is now an adult scientist working with Professor Brand to solve a gravity equation needed to launch Earth's population. Brand is aging rapidly; on his deathbed he admits he lied about having a workable Plan A and that Plan B is the real hope.

With limited fuel, the crew chooses to visit Mann's ice world because Mann's transmission is most recent. On Mann's planet they discover his transmitted data was falsified: Dr. Mann has been lying about habitability to be rescued. Mann attempts to steal a shuttle and abandons Cooper by puncturing his suit; Romilly dies when Mann detonates a device on Endurance. Mann docks improperly with Endurance; the resulting decompression kills Mann and damages the ship. Cooper pilots a crippled shuttle to stabilize Endurance and, together with Amelia, uses a slingshot maneuver around Gargantua to direct Endurance toward Edmunds' planet. To provide the required propulsion and obtain data from the black hole's singularity needed to complete Brand's gravity equation, Cooper and TARS detach and dive into Gargantua, while Amelia continues to Edmunds.

Instead of destruction, Cooper and TARS emerge into a four-dimensional construct — a tesseract — built inside the black hole where time is presented spatially. Cooper recognizes the construct contains scenes from Murph's childhood bedroom across different moments. He realizes the "ghost" was him, guided by future humans who engineered the wormhole to allow this intervention. Cooper uses gravitational manipulation in the tesseract to transmit quantum-gravity data (collected by TARS from inside the singularity) to Murph by encoding it in the second hand of her old watch as binary Morse. Murph, now an adult NASA scientist, deciphers the data, completes Brand's equation, and enables humanity's evacuation from Earth onto space stations.

After transmitting the data, the tesseract collapses and Cooper is ejected from the black hole; he is rescued and awakens on Cooper Station, a cylindrical space habitat near Saturn. There he reunites with an elderly Murph, who is near death; she tells him to seek Amelia, who transported herself to Edmunds to begin Plan B. The film ends with Cooper stealing a small spacecraft to travel toward Edmunds to find Amelia.
original_full_tokens.setting: Near-future Earth (Midwest and NASA near Saturn) and another galaxy via wormhole

original_full_tokens.plot_summary:
In 2049 Los Angeles, LAPD blade runner KD6-3.7, called K, a Nexus-9 replicant, locates and 'retires' rogue replicant Sapper Morton at his protein farm. In Morton's yard K finds a buried wooden box containing the remains of a female replicant who died during childbirth and an encoded date carved into a tree: 6-10-21. Lieutenant Joshi orders K to recover and destroy any evidence and locate the child, because proof that replicants can reproduce could spark war. K runs the remains through DNA archives at the Wallace Corporation and learns the corpse is Rachael, a Nexus-7 who disappeared with former blade runner Rick Deckard in 2019. Niander Wallace, CEO of Wallace Corporation, wants the reproduction secret; he sends his enforcer Luv to monitor K. K recalls a childhood memory — a wooden toy horse and an orphanage — and believes it connects him to the case. His holographic companion Joi encourages the idea that K might be Rachael's child, because implanted memories that feel real could mean he was born, not manufactured.

K tracks orphanage records and finds two birth entries for June 10, 2021 with identical DNA except sex chromosomes; only the boy is listed as living. K locates the living child, takes him briefly to an orphanage, and later, after visiting memory designer Dr. Ana Stelline in San Diego and failing a baseline test, learns Stelline confirms the authenticity of the orphanage memory. Believing he is Rachael and Deckard's son, K becomes obsessed; Lieutenant Joshi gives him 48 hours to pass his next baseline or be retired.

To get closer to leads in the Los Angeles projects, K hires replicant sex worker Mariette to appear as Joi so he can have sex with the real Mariette; Mariette, at Joi's prompting, plants a tracker on K. The tracker lets the Replicant Freedom Movement (aka Replicant Underground) locate K when he is ambushed. K deciphers the toy horse's origin to an irradiated Las Vegas and travels there, encountering the Underground’s leader Freysa. In Las Vegas, K finds Deckard living in hiding; Deckard reveals he and Rachael had a child and that Deckard scrambled the birth records to conceal the child's identity, leaving the child with the Underground. Deckard refuses to reveal the child's location.

Luv, pursuing K, kills Lieutenant Joshi and then follows K to Las Vegas. Luv captures Deckard and delivers him to Wallace, who wants Deckard to reveal the child's whereabouts; Wallace offers Deckard a fabricated Rachael in exchange. Deckard refuses; Luv murders the replica Rachael. K fights Luv, is beaten and left for dead, but is rescued by the Replicant Underground using Mariette's tracker. Freysa tells K that Rachael's child is actually a daughter — Dr. Ana Stelline — not the boy K found. The Underground expected K to kill Deckard to prevent Wallace from getting Stelline; they ask K to do it. K refuses.

Luv abducts Deckard aboard a Wallace transport bound off-world. K intercepts, battles Luv on the transport, and drowns Luv in the sea but suffers severe injuries. K stages Deckard's death to protect him, takes Deckard to Dr. Ana Stelline's facility in Los Angeles, and gives Deckard the wooden toy horse as proof. Deckard enters Stelline's clean-room; he sees his grown daughter, Ana Stelline, for the first time and embraces her. K, mortally wounded, collapses on the steps outside in falling synthetic snow and dies as Deckard looks on.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary: On Isla Nublar, industrialist John Hammond has built Jurassic Park, a theme park populated by cloned dinosaurs created from DNA recovered from amber. After a worker is killed by a velociraptor, Hammond invites paleontologist Dr. Alan Grant and paleobotanist Dr. Ellie Sattler, along with chaos theorist Dr. Ian Malcolm and lawyer Donald Gennaro, to inspect and certify the park for investors. At the visitor center they see living dinosaurs (including a brachiosaurus) and witness a velociraptor hatching; Dr. Henry Wu explains gaps in the genomes were filled with frog DNA and claims all specimens were engineered female. Hammond's grandchildren, Lex and Tim Murphy, join the group for a guided electric tour overseen from the control room. During the tour a sick triceratops delays Sattler and the approaching tropical storm forces most staff to evacuate to the mainland. Lead programmer Dennis Nedry, bribed to steal fertilized embryos, disables the park's security systems to access the embryo storage, deactivating electric fences and tour vehicles as he leaves the control room. Stranded near the Tyrannosaurus rex paddock by the outage, the tour vehicles are attacked: the T. rex destroys a vehicle, kills Gennaro, injures Malcolm, and Grant escapes into the park with Lex and Tim. Nedry becomes lost in the rain, crashes, and is killed by a dilophosaurus after his embryo canister is stolen by a Komodo-like thief (in his jeep). Hammond, Sattler and staff learn Nedry locked the system; Raymond Arnold attempts a hard reboot but the shutdown opens other fences and releases velociraptors. Muldoon and Sattler search the maintenance sheds; Muldoon distracts raptors and is killed, Sattler finds Arnold's severed arm and narrowly survives a raptor attack. Grant, Lex and Tim shelter in trees, discover broken eggs and realize dinosaurs have been breeding because frog DNA allowed sex changes. Grant finds Tim electrocuted on a fence and resuscitates him with CPR. The visitor center becomes a refuge; Grant goes searching for Sattler and returns with her. Lex, who can use the park's computer, restores power in the control room, allowing Hammond to call for help. As the group attempts to flee through the park entrance, they are cornered by velociraptors in the visitor center kitchen; a returning Tyrannosaurus rex attacks and kills the raptors, giving the survivors time to reach the rendezvous. Hammond, Malcolm and the others arrive; the survivors board a helicopter and leave Isla Nublar.

major_characters.primary_motivations: Ensure his safety and the children’s survival while investigating how the dinosaurs behave.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary: Twelve oval alien spacecraft appear simultaneously around the world; one lands in Montana. The U.S. Army assembles a small research team at the Montana site led by Colonel G.T. Weber who recruits linguist Dr. Louise Banks and physicist Ian Donnelly to decipher the aliens' purpose. Inside the craft humans encounter two seven-limbed heptapods (nicknamed Abbott and Costello) that produce complex circular written logograms. Louise insists on direct, repeated interaction to build a translation system; she removes protective gear to get closer and compiles the heptapod writing into a shared lexicon with Ian. As she studies their written language she begins experiencing vivid personal visions of a little girl growing up and dying of cancer. Military and political tensions mount worldwide after an early translation of the heptapods' phrase “offer weapon” (also read elsewhere as “use weapon”) sparks fear; China and other nations threaten or prepare attacks and international collaboration fractures. Louise and Ian are inside the ship when rogue soldiers detonate explosives; both are ejected and knocked unconscious but survive. Back at camp, Ian discovers that the heptapod logograms encode a radically different relation to time—references to a non-linear perception and a recurring symbol for time present across messages. Louise realizes the heptapods' written language rearranges cognition so that learners experience future events as memories. She returns alone to the craft where Costello explains (via gestures and new logograms) that the heptapods have come to give humanity their language as a “tool” (misread earlier as “weapon”)—a gift that will enable humans to help the heptapods three thousand years in the future. Louise understands her visions are flash-forwards: the little girl she has been seeing will be her future daughter, Hannah, who does not yet exist and will later die young. In a future vision at a U.N. reception General Shang thanks Louise for convincing him not to attack; he reveals she had called him on his private number and repeated his late wife's last words in Mandarin, which persuaded him. In the present Louise steals a satellite phone, places the call to Shang and—using the Mandarin phrase from her vision—convinces him to stand down; China calls off its attack and global cooperation resumes. The aliens depart peacefully. Louise later publishes a book on the heptapod language (The Universal Language). Ian confesses love to Louise; she chooses to have a child with him despite knowing, from her acquired non-linear knowledge, that their daughter Hannah will be born, die of cancer at age twelve, and that Ian will eventually leave them. The film ends with Louise holding Hannah as a child, fully aware of the future she has accepted.
original_full_tokens.setting: Contemporary, multiple global sites; primary location: Montana, USA

major_characters.primary_motivations: To understand and translate the aliens' language to determine their intentions and prevent global conflict; she pursues communication because accurate interpretation can avert war.

major_characters.primary_motivations: To collaborate with Louise to decipher the heptapod language and understand the scientific implications of contact; he becomes personally attached to Louise.

major_characters.primary_motivations: To provide humanity with their written language (a cognitive tool) so humans can later help the heptapods; their goal drives first-contact communication.

major_characters.primary_motivations: Initially prepared to attack the heptapods out of national security concerns; his decision to stand down prevents global war.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary:
At his grandmother Ellen Taper Leigh’s funeral, 78-year-old Ellen’s daughter Annie Graham delivers the eulogy while Annie’s husband Steve wakes their teenage son Peter and 13-year-old daughter Charlie. Charlie makes a strange clucking noise and draws a repeated symbol. That night Annie finds a memory book and briefly sees Ellen’s face. At school a bird smashes into Charlie’s classroom window; Charlie beheads the bird. Charlie later asks Annie if Ellen always wanted Charlie to be a boy and says she wants Ellen. Annie attends a grief support group and learns members practice séance techniques; Joan, a group member, shows Annie how to conduct a household summoning using a personal item, an incantation, and the whole family present.

Peter promises to take Charlie to a party but leaves her unsupervised; Charlie eats cake containing an allergen, suffers anaphylaxis and, while Peter drives her to the hospital at night, Charlie’s head is severed when her head snaps out the car window and hits a utility pole. The Grahams hold a funeral for Charlie. Annie spirals into guilt and sleepwalking; she confesses to Peter she once tried to induce a miscarriage and shows signs of dissociation. She constructs a miniature diorama of Charlie’s death.

Annie tries Joan’s séance method with Charlie’s sketchbook and personal items; the family briefly appears to contact Charlie, but Annie becomes possessed or appears to be by Charlie/Ellen and Peter witnesses terrifying phenomena in his room (a reflection that doesn’t match him, a light above the bed, and visions of Charlie’s head falling off). Annie tells Peter she is the only one who can stop the spirit. She discovers in Joan’s apartment evidence linking Ellen and Joan to a coven devoted to the demon Paimon and to rituals that intended to host Paimon in a male human body; photographs show a triangular seal and images of Peter’s face inside ceremonial diagrams.

Annie finds Ellen’s decapitated corpse in the attic. She confronts Steve and demands he burn Charlie’s sketchbook to save Peter; when Steve refuses, Annie throws the book into the fire and Steve spontaneously combusts. Annie, now fully possessed, stalks Peter through the house. She hovers and then severs her own head. Peter flees to the attic, finds candles, flies, and a picture of his face with the eyes gouged out; Ellen’s body is missing. Peter jumps out of the tree house window to escape; his head strikes the ground and he appears briefly stunned.

Peter awakens disoriented in the tree house where a naked, mostly disrobed coven waits. Charlie’s severed head is mounted on a statue of Paimon; Joan and other coven members crown Peter and call him by Charlie’s name, welcoming Paimon into a male vessel. The film ends on one of Annie’s miniature dioramas: a model tree house populated by dolls representing Peter (now Paimon’s host), the coven, and the headless corpses of Annie and Ellen.
original_full_tokens.setting: Contemporary suburban Colorado (family home, tree house, church, attic)

major_characters.primary_motivations: To contact/restore her daughter Charlie and control the family’s grief, driven by guilt and a need for closure.

major_characters.description: Grief-group member and coven operative who instructs Annie in summoning techniques

original_full_tokens.plot_summary: Jack Torrance, a recovering alcoholic and former teacher, interviews with Overlook Hotel manager Stuart Ullman in Colorado and accepts the winter caretaker job to write and provide for his wife Wendy and son Danny. Ullman warns Jack about isolation and recounts a past caretaker, Charles Grady, who murdered his family and himself. Danny, who has a psychic ability called the "shining," collapses on the way to the hotel and later meets head chef Dick Hallorann, who recognizes Danny's gift and warns him specifically to stay out of Room 237. The staff leaves as winter sets in and the Torrances are snowbound. Jack suffers writer's block, insomnia and increasing irritability. Danny experiences visions: an elevator spouting blood, the two murdered Grady girls, and other disturbing images. Danny repeatedly senses a presence tied to the hotel's rooms; at one point a ball rolls from Room 237 and Danny later appears bruised and disoriented after entering it. Jack begins seeing apparitions in the hotel — he encounters bartender Lloyd in the empty Gold Room and later a lavish 1920s party where Delbert Grady (the former caretaker) tells Jack that Danny must be "corrected" and instructs Jack how to handle Wendy. Jack's behavior grows erratic: he denies problems in Room 237 despite seeing a woman who transforms into a rotting corpse there, lashes out verbally at Wendy, and reverts toward violence. Wendy discovers Jack's manuscript is pages of the repeated line "All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy," proving his mental collapse. When Wendy confronts him he attacks; she incapacitates and locks him in the pantry. Jack sabotages the radio and the snowcat to prevent escape. Danny, channeling his psychic trauma, writes "REDRUM" and speaks hypnotically; in a mirror the word reads "MURDER." Jack breaks out and relentlessly pursues Wendy and Danny, chopping through doors with an axe and burning the hotel further into murderous frenzy. Dick Hallorann senses Danny's distress from Florida and flies to the Overlook; Jack murders Hallorann with an axe when Hallorann arrives. Danny hides in a cabinet, escapes to the outside and leads Jack into the hedge maze. Using foresight and care, Danny retraces his footprints to confuse Jack; Jack becomes lost, freezes to death in the snowbound maze. Wendy and Danny escape in the snowcat. The film closes on the hotel's historical photo wall: a 1921 photograph shows Jack among the hotel's past guests, implying Jack has become part of the Overlook's history and influence.
original_full_tokens.setting: Early 1980s, The Overlook Hotel — secluded Colorado mountains in winter

major_characters.name: Jack Torrance
major_characters.description: recovering alcoholic, failed teacher turned aspiring writer; Overlook winter caretaker
major_characters.primary_motivations: To secure the caretaker job and write a novel so he can rebuild his life and provide for his family; his isolation and susceptibility to the hotel's influence push him toward violence.

major_characters.name: Wendy Torrance
major_characters.description: Jack's wife, protective mother
major_characters.primary_motivations: To keep her son Danny safe and survive the escalating danger posed by Jack and the hotel's supernatural forces.

major_characters.name: Danny Torrance
major_characters.description: their young son with psychic 'shining' abilities
major_characters.primary_motivations: To survive the hotel's attacks and use his psychic ability to warn and escape, partly by summoning help from Dick Hallorann.

major_characters.name: Dick Hallorann
major_characters.description: Overlook head chef who also 'shines' and recognizes Danny's gift
major_characters.primary_motivations: To protect Danny after sensing his psychic distress and to help rescue the Torrances from the hotel's malevolent forces.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary: Renai and Josh Lambert move into a new suburban house with their three children: Dalton, Foster and their infant daughter. One night Dalton climbs into the attic, falls from a ladder and thereafter sleeps in an unresponsive, inexplicable coma. Three months later Dalton is returned home; soon strange phenomena escalate: whispering on the baby monitor (“I want it now!”), a bloody handprint on Dalton’s bed, a frightening man seen in the baby’s room, and Foster saying Dalton “walks around” at night. After Renai is assaulted by the strange man, the family moves, but the hauntings follow and intensify—Lorraine (Josh’s mother) dreams of a presence in Dalton’s room and later sees a red-faced figure behind Josh. Lorraine contacts paranormal investigator Elise Rainier. Elise and her two assistants visit Dalton’s room; Elise identifies a black figure with a red face and hollow eyes and explains Dalton’s ability to astral-projection: while his body sleeps his consciousness travels, and this time Dalton has wandered too far into “The Further,” a realm of lost souls. Tormented spirits and a malicious red-faced demon now seek to possess Dalton’s body. Josh reluctantly accepts Elise’s claim after discovering Dalton’s drawings match Elise’s description. During a séance Dalton’s spirit briefly appears but the demon uses Dalton’s body to lash out, and multiple entities assault the group. Elise reveals to Josh that he, too, can astral-project and was terrorized as a child; family photos show a shadowy old woman looming closer behind young Josh. Elise prepares Josh to enter the Further to find Dalton. In astral form Josh sees his sleeping body and the team in the physical world, then travels through a misty, desolate landscape. He follows clues—a boy who points him toward a house (the Lamperts’ original home), a living-room scene where a smiling female member of a spectral family shoots a family—then reaches the attic and a red lacquered door. He defeats the violent man apparition and enters the door into the Further. Inside a cavernous red chamber Josh finds Dalton chained and sobbing; he frees him but the red-faced demon attacks. Josh and Dalton flee toward their bodies while the demon pursues. Josh briefly confronts the shadowy old woman inside the House and screams that he isn’t afraid; she retreats. Josh and Dalton awaken in their bodies; the spirits vanish. The family appears reunited and Elise prepares to leave with Josh. When Elise takes Josh’s childhood photos, she snaps a photo of Josh; he suddenly becomes violent, assaults and strangles Elise to death, then disappears. Renai finds Elise’s camera and discovers a picture of the shadowy old woman; Elise’s last observation—Josh’s old, dirt-stained hand—implies the woman has possessed Josh. The film ends with Josh, now possessed, placing his hand on Renai’s shoulder saying “Renai, I’m right here,” while the shadowy-old-woman presence is shown blowing out a candle in a post-credits shot.
original_full_tokens.setting: Modern-day suburban American homes and the Otherworld “The Further”

major_characters.name: Josh Lambert
major_characters.description: Father who discovers he can astral-project and enters the Further to rescue his son.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Find and return Dalton from the Further to save his son and family.

major_characters.name: Renai Lambert
major_characters.description: Josh’s wife and Dalton’s mother, experiences most of the physical hauntings.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Protect her children and restore Dalton to health.

major_characters.name: Dalton Lambert
major_characters.description: Their young son in an unexplained coma whose spirit astral-projects into the Further.
major_characters.primary_motivations: (Unconscious) His astral-projections unintentionally travel too far and leave his body vulnerable.

major_characters.name: Elise Rainier
major_characters.description: Experienced paranormal investigator and medium who leads the attempt to retrieve Dalton.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Enter or guide someone into the Further to retrieve Dalton and stop the hostile spirits.

major_characters.name: The Shadowy Old Woman (the Lipstick-Face Demon)
major_characters.description: Malevolent spirit first seen in photos and manifesting as a shadowy old woman and a red-faced demonic figure.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Use or possess a living body (ultimately Josh’s) to return to the physical world and feed on life.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary: After being decapitated by Sienna Shaw, Art the Clown's headless body kills a responding police officer and is taken to an asylum where Victoria Heyes—recent survivor possessed by the Little Pale Girl—has just given birth to Art's head. Art and Victoria kill a nurse and a guard and flee to an abandoned house. Victoria slashes her wrists and enters a bathtub hibernation while Art sits dormant in the attic. Five years later Sienna is released from a psychiatric facility and moves in with her Aunt Jess, uncle Greg, and niece Gabbie in Miles County. Sienna suffers hallucinations of her dead friend Brooke and struggles to reconnect with her younger brother Jonathan, now a college student. Two demolition workers discover the abandoned house and awaken Art and Victoria; they kill the workers. Victoria begins making a costume while Art resumes killing. At a bar Art meets a Santa impersonator, shoots the bartender and a patron, steals a Santa suit, murders the impersonator using liquid nitrogen and a hammer, then later axes a family at their home while dressed as Santa. Art impersonates Santa at a shopping mall and hides explosives in gifts, detonating them to slaughter shoppers and children. Sienna briefly thinks she sees Art at the mall but keeps quiet, unsure if it's a hallucination. Sienna visits Jonathan at university and meets his roommate Cole and Cole's girlfriend Mia, who hosts a true-crime podcast. Mia pressures Sienna and Jonathan for an interview; Sienna lashes out and later tells Jonathan she believes Art is reborn and possessed others, citing letters Jonathan sent her from the hospital about demons reborn through recently deceased evil humans. Art attacks the university, killing Mia and Cole with a chainsaw in the showers. News of the mall bombing reaches Sienna; she panics and tells Jonathan to come home. Greg leaves to fetch Jonathan. Sienna dreams of angels forging armor from her father's warrior costume design, then wakes to find Art and Victoria in the house. Art incapacitates her with a mallet. She awakens to Greg's decapitated body pinned to the wall, his head used as the Christmas tree's star, and Jess tied up. Victoria presents a skinless head she claims is Gabbie's, forces rats to eat Jess by hammering a tube into her mouth and using a blowtorch, then slits Jess's throat. Art returns with Gabbie as a hostage and reveals the decapitated head is Jonathan's. Victoria crowns Sienna with thorns and attempts to possess her, but Sienna resists. Sienna tricks them into letting her open a wrapped "present," then stabs Victoria with her father's sword and decapitates her; Victoria's body disintegrates. Victoria's blood burns through the floor and opens a portal; Gabbie slips and falls in, clutching the sword. Sienna rushes to save Gabbie but gabbie falls into the abyss. Art attacks with a chainsaw; Sienna gains the upper hand and wounds heal supernaturally. Art flees through a window and escapes on a bus. Sienna vows to find and rescue Gabbie as Art rides away, presumably still alive.
original_full_tokens.setting: Modern-day Miles County around Christmas, present day

major_characters.name: Sienna Shaw
major_characters.description: Survivor of Art's massacre, released from psychiatric care and living with relatives.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Protect her family and stop Art after surviving his previous massacre; she seeks to atone for Brooke's death and rescue loved ones.

major_characters.name: Art the Clown
major_characters.description: Sadistic supernatural killer clown who returns from decapitation to massacre again.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Unleash violent, ritualistic murders and spread terror; his rebirth drives him to resume killing.

major_characters.name: Victoria Heyes
major_characters.description: Survivor possessed by the Little Pale Girl who births Art's head and later becomes his companion/host.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Enable Art's return by serving as a vessel and accomplice, perpetuating possession and violence.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary: Photographer Adam Faulkner wakes up in a bathtub in a grimy industrial bathroom; as the water drains a small glowing object is briefly seen disappear down the plughole. Adam discovers he is chained by the ankle to a pipe at one corner of the room. On the opposite side Dr. Lawrence Gordon, a surgeon, is likewise chained; between them lies a dead man clutching a revolver and a micro-cassette player. Each man finds a cassette in his pocket. Playing them, Adam hears a taunting voice calling him a voyeur and demanding he act; Lawrence's tape orders him to kill Adam by 6:00 or his wife Alison and daughter will be killed and he will remain chained forever. They find hacksaws hidden in the toilet tank but discover the saws are too dull to cut metal chain; they deduce the saws are meant to sever their own ankles. Detectives David Tapp and Steven Sing are investigating previous victims of the same killer (dubbed the "Jigsaw" killer) and question Amanda Young, the only known survivor, in flashbacks. Flashbacks also show earlier Jigsaw traps (Paul and Mark) and an interrupted attempt by Tapp and Sing to capture the killer, during which Tapp is slashed and Sing later dies in a booby-trapped house. Back in the bathroom, Lawrence and Adam find a hidden camera and later a box containing cigarettes, a lighter, a note, and a cell phone. The note suggests Lawrence dip a cigarette in poisoned blood and use it to kill Adam; Lawrence and Adam stage Adam's fake death to trick the camera, but an electric shock proves Adam is still alive. Alison calls Lawrence on the cell phone and tells him Adam knows more than he has admitted; Adam admits he was hired by Tapp to spy on Lawrence and has photographs showing an orderly named Zep outside Lawrence's house. At six o'clock Alison attempts to fight off Zep, who has entered her house; she is overpowered but manages to arm herself briefly before Tapp arrives and wounds Zep. Zep flees toward the bathroom. Lawrence, desperate to obey his tape and save his family, uses the hacksaw to sever his own right foot at the ankle, constrains himself to the floor enough to reach the revolver from the dead man's hand, and shoots Adam in the abdomen. Zep bursts into the bathroom and shoots Tapp fatally when Tapp pursues him; Zep tells Lawrence "it's the rules." Adam, still alive, kills Zep with the toilet tank lid. Lawrence drags himself out promising to return with help; Adam searches Zep and finds another cassette player. The tape reveals Zep was also forced to follow orders. The supposedly dead man in the center of the room removes a latex piece that faked his head wound and stands up: he is John Kramer, a terminally ill patient previously treated by Dr. Gordon and the real Jigsaw. John speaks weakly from a hidden monitor, explains the key to Adam's chain was in the bathtub and had been lost down the drain at the film's start, and reiterates his philosophy about forcing victims to appreciate life. John delivers a final taunting speech, stuns Adam with an electrical device, plays a montage of the night's events, slams the bathroom door and locks it, sealing Adam inside as John declares "game over."
original_full_tokens.setting: Present-day dilapidated industrial bathroom and suburban locations

major_characters.name: Adam Faulkner
major_characters.description: Freelance photographer who woke chained in the bathroom.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Survive the bathroom trap and uncover why he was targeted after being hired to spy on Lawrence.

major_characters.name: Dr. Lawrence Gordon
major_characters.description: A surgeon chained opposite Adam whose family are threatened.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Obey the tape's order to kill Adam by 6:00 to save his wife Alison and daughter, driven by fear for his family.

major_characters.name: John Kramer (Jigsaw)
major_characters.description: Terminally ill man who orchestrates elaborate "games" forcing victims to survive by brutal choices.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Force victims to prove they value life by surviving his tests and thus punish perceived complacency.

major_characters.name: Detective David Tapp
major_characters.description: Obsessive police detective investigating the Jigsaw murders.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Catch Jigsaw for justice and to avenge his partner Sing, driven to surveillance and obsession.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary:
In an unnamed, rain-soaked city, veteran homicide lieutenant William Somerset is days from retirement when the arrogant but idealistic Detective David Mills is assigned to his precinct. Somerset and Mills investigate a grisly murder of an obese man found forced to eat himself to death; the word "GLUTTONY" is written on the wall. They are alerted to a second crime scene: a prominent defense attorney murdered with a pound of his flesh removed and the word "GREED" scrawled. Forensic clues (an upside-down picture and hidden fingerprints) lead Somerset and Mills to an apartment where they find a severely emaciated drug dealer and child molester who has been held and photographed over a year — the victim of "SLOTH." Library records and detective work link the crimes to a man using the name John Doe. Mills chases Doe across rooftops; Mills falls and is later knocked unconscious, letting Doe escape briefly. The detectives search Doe's apartment and find notebooks detailing meticulous planning and a clue to a subsequent murder. They arrive too late to prevent Doe from forcing a man at gunpoint to rape and kill a prostitute ("LUST"). Next the detectives find a mutilated model who chose suicide over living disfigured ("PRIDE").

Doe unexpectedly surrenders to police, covered in blood. He offers to confess but insists on escorting Somerset and Mills to a remote location where he will reveal the last two victims ("ENVY" and "WRATH"). During the drive Doe calmly lectures them about morality, revealing his crimes are designed as righteous punishment using the seven deadly sins. At the deserted drop site a delivery van arrives with a sealed box. Somerset opens it and sees something that panics him; Doe declares he represents ENVY, saying he envied Mills' marriage and life. Doe taunts Mills by saying the box contains the severed head of Mills' wife, Tracy, and that she begged for her life and their unborn child. Somerset pleads for Mills not to react, but Mills, consumed by fury, shoots Doe multiple times, enacting "WRATH." Doe dies having orchestrated both his own death and Mills' transformation into the final sin. Police take a broken Mills away; Somerset, who had planned to retire, decides to stay on. The film ends with Somerset's voiceover quoting Hemingway: "the world is a fine place and worth fighting for," and adds, "I agree with the second part."
original_full_tokens.setting: Unnamed modern rainy metropolis (contemporary city)

major_characters.name: William Somerset
major_characters.description: A seasoned, soon-to-retire homicide lieutenant in the unnamed city
major_characters.primary_motivations: To solve the escalating murders, understand the killer's method, and maintain order before retiring.

major_characters.name: David Mills
major_characters.description: A hot-headed, idealistic young detective recently transferred to the precinct
major_characters.primary_motivations: To prove himself as a cop, protect his wife Tracy, and stop the killer quickly.

major_characters.name: John Doe
major_characters.description: A meticulously planning serial killer who stages murders around the seven deadly sins
major_characters.primary_motivations: To punish sinners and shock society by arranging murders that embody each of the seven deadly sins and to manipulate the detectives into completing his final plan.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary: The Kim family—father Kim Ki-taek, mother Choong-sook, son Ki-woo, and daughter Ki-jeong—live in a semi-basement apartment in Seoul and scrape by doing low-paid odd jobs. Ki-woo's college friend Min-hyuk prepares to study abroad and gives Ki-woo a chance: take Min-hyuk's job tutoring Park Da-hye, daughter of the wealthy Park family, while Min-hyuk is away. Ki-woo accepts, using a forged university degree created by Ki-jeong. At the Parks' modernist house (former architect Namgoong's residence), Ki-woo impresses Mrs. Park and is hired. Learning Mrs. Park needs an art therapist for young Park Da-song, Ki-woo recommends 'Jessica'—Ki-jeong in disguise—who is hired. Ki-jeong engineers the dismissal of the Parks' driver by framing him for sexual harassment, then arranges for Ki-taek to be hired as the new driver. The Kims then manipulate Mrs. Park into firing longtime housekeeper Moon-gwang by falsely suggesting Moon-gwang has tuberculosis; Choong-sook is hired as housekeeper, completing the family's infiltration of the household staff. The Kims enjoy the comfort of the house; Ki-woo and Da-hye begin a romantic connection. While the Parks go on a camping weekend, the Kims throw a drunken party in the mansion. Moon-gwang unexpectedly returns to retrieve something from the basement and reveals a secret bunker where her husband, Geun-sae, has been hiding for four years to escape loan sharks. Moon-gwang records the Kims' true identities and threatens to expose them. The Parks cut their trip short because of heavy rain; the Kims frantically try to hide Moon-gwang and Geun-sae. In a scuffle, Choong-sook pushes Moon-gwang down basement stairs, inflicting a fatal head wound. Moon-gwang later dies of her injury; her husband is freed when Moon-gwang cuts the tape binding him. The storm floods the Kims' apartment; they sleep in a gymnasium while the Parks throw a birthday party for Da-song the next day and ask the staff to assist. Ki-woo takes the 'lucky' rock given earlier by Min-hyuk down to the bunker intending to kill Geun-sae; Geun-sae ambushes Ki-woo, bludgeons him with the rock, then bursts into the party and stabs Ki-jeong in the chest. Da-song's resulting seizure creates chaos; as staff and Parks struggle, Choong-sook fatally stabs Geun-sae with a meat skewer. Mr. Park recoils at Geun-sae's smell; witnessing Mr. Park's disgust and the class contempt in his reaction, Ki-taek snaps and stabs Mr. Park to death, then flees. In the aftermath Ki-jeong dies from her wound; Ki-woo undergoes brain surgery and a coma; Ki-taek evades capture by hiding in the bunker. Ki-woo and Choong-sook face trial and receive probation. Months later Ki-woo, recovering in the semi-basement, watches the Park house and sees a light flickering in Morse code from the basement—the message is from Ki-taek, who survives hidden in the bunker under the house. Ki-woo vows to earn enough money to buy the house and free his father; the film ends with Ki-woo imagining reuniting with Ki-taek while remaining trapped in poverty.
original_full_tokens.setting: Modern-day Seoul; Parks' modernist mansion and Kim semi-basement

major_characters.name: Kim Ki-taek
major_characters.description: Unemployed patriarch who becomes the Park family's driver.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Provide for his family and find security after years of humiliation and poverty.

major_characters.name: Choong-sook (Kim Choong-sook)
major_characters.description: Ki-taek's wife who becomes the Park housekeeper.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Protect her family and secure comfortable work and living conditions.

major_characters.name: Kim Ki-woo
major_characters.description: College-age son who poses as an English tutor for the Parks.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Escape poverty by gaining access to wealth and opportunities through the Parks.

major_characters.name: Kim Ki-jeong

major_characters.primary_motivations: Help her family ascend socially and financially by any means necessary.

major_characters.description: Wealthy, status-conscious patriarch of the Park family.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Maintain his family's comfort and social status, indifferent to the lower classes' realities.

major_characters.name: Mrs. Park
major_characters.description: Flighty, naive matriarch of the Park family.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Keep her family happy and preserve appearances, relying on staff to manage details.

major_characters.name: Moon-gwang
major_characters.description: Longtime housekeeper who originally worked for the house's former owner.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Reclaim her job and protect her hidden husband, Geun-sae, from exposure.

major_characters.description: Moon-gwang's husband who has lived hidden in the house's bunker for years.
major_characters.primary_motivations: Survive in hiding to avoid loan sharks and remain with his wife.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary:
Photographer Chris Washington flies to the Armitage family estate with his white girlfriend Rose Armitage to meet her parents, neurosurgeon Dean Armitage and psychiatrist/hypnotherapist Missy Armitage, and brother Jeremy. On the drive upstate they hit a deer; a police officer questions Chris despite not driving, which Rose later frames as racial profiling. At the Armitage house Chris notices odd behavior from the Black housekeeper Georgina and groundskeeper Walter, and the family makes uncomfortable remarks about Black people and athletic figures. Missy persuades Chris to undergo hypnotherapy to stop smoking; under hypnosis he relives his mother's hit-and-run death and sinks into Missy’s “sunken place,” a dissociative trance she uses as a control technique. Georgina unplugs Chris’s phone overnight. The Armitages host an annual garden party attended by wealthy white guests who fetishize Black bodies; Chris meets blind art dealer Jim Hudson and a nervous Black man who calls himself Logan King. Chris learns from his friend Rod Williams that “Logan” is actually Andre, a missing man from Brooklyn. Chris covertly photographs Andre; the flash triggers a panicked seizure. Later Chris finds old photos showing Rose with other Black partners, including Walter and Georgina, contradicting her claim he’s her first Black boyfriend.

When Chris decides to leave, Rose refuses to give him the keys; Jeremy blocks the door. Missy hypnotizes Chris again and sends him to the sunken place, then Chris awakens bound to a surgical chair in a basement operating room. The family reveals their true operation: Dean and an inner circle perform neurosurgical transplants that transfer the minds of wealthy white clients (including Rose’s grandfather Roman) into the bodies of young Black people while the original consciousness is imprisoned in the sunken place. Jim Hudson explains he wants Chris’s eyes and artistic vision; Rose’s role is to lure Black partners. Rod, suspicious, goes to the police but is dismissed.

As staff prepare Chris for surgery, he plugs his ears to resist Missy’s trigger and fights back. He seizes Jeremy, bludgeons him, and during a struggle kills Dean (who falls and ignites a fire) and Missy. He finishes Jeremy after Jeremy ambushes him. Chris flees in Jeremy’s Porsche but strikes Georgina—who secretly houses Rose’s grandmother Marianne’s mind; she attacks and dies in the crash. Rose and Walter (whose body houses Roman’s mind) pursue Chris. Chris uses his phone flash to briefly free Walter from Roman’s control; Walter shoots Rose and then fatally shoots himself. Chris, unable to kill Rose, surrenders when a police car approaches—only to discover the car is driven by Rod, who had tracked and rescued him.
original_full_tokens.setting: Contemporary upstate country estate and surrounding roads

major_characters.name: Chris Washington
major_characters.description: Young African-American photographer visiting Rose's family; victim-turned-survivor
major_characters.primary_motivations: Escape the Armitage estate alive and survive after realizing he is targeted for a brain transplant.

major_characters.name: Rose Armitage
major_characters.description: White girlfriend who lures Black partners to her family
major_characters.primary_motivations: Facilitate her family's transplantation scheme by attracting Black partners and maintaining the ruse.

major_characters.name: Dean Armitage
major_characters.description: White neurosurgeon patriarch who performs transplants
major_characters.primary_motivations: Acquire Black bodies to house white clients' consciousnesses to extend status and power.

major_characters.name: Missy Armitage
major_characters.description: Rose's mother, psychiatrist and hypnotist who controls victims
major_characters.primary_motivations: Use hypnosis to subdue victims into the 'sunken place' enabling the family's surgeries.

major_characters.name: Rod Williams
major_characters.description: Chris's friend, TSA officer and ally
major_characters.primary_motivations: Find and rescue Chris after becoming suspicious of the Armitages' treatment of him.

original_full_tokens.plot_summary: In 1987 New York City, 27-year-old investment banker Patrick Bateman maintains a meticulous daily grooming routine and social life among wealthy Wall Street colleagues while hiding violent urges. Engaged to Evelyn, Bateman has an affair with Courtney and despises coworkers like Paul Allen. After becoming enraged by Paul Allen's superior business card, Bateman murders a homeless man and the man's dog, then later gets Paul drunk, brings him to his Manhattan apartment, and kills him with an axe while playing Huey Lewis's "Hip to Be Square." Bateman stages Paul's apartment to suggest Paul fled to London. Private detective Donald Kimball interviews Bateman about Paul's disappearance. Bateman lures two prostitutes, Christie and Sabrina, to his apartment, introduces himself as Paul Allen, tortures and injures them, and later has sex with Christie and Elizabeth in Paul's apartment before killing Elizabeth; Christie flees, discovers multiple female corpses, and is chased and killed when Bateman drops a chainsaw down the stairwell. Bateman repeatedly attempts and sometimes follows through on murders: he tries to kill coworker Luis Carruthers in a restaurant restroom but flees when Luis misreads the attack as a sexual advance; he murders a model; he holds a nail gun to his secretary Jean's head when she visits his apartment and then asks her to leave. Bateman breaks off his engagement to Evelyn. Wandering the city, Bateman encounters a stray kitten at an ATM, is seen trying to shoot it, shoots a woman who intervenes, then engages in a gunfight with arriving police, destroying patrol cars and killing officers before fleeing into office buildings. In the wrong building he murders a security guard and a janitor, then calls his lawyer Harold and leaves a lengthy, explicit confession on Harold's answering machine, falsely identifying himself as Paul Allen in other interactions. The next day Bateman returns to Paul's apartment expecting gore, but finds it clean and for sale; his colleagues treat Paul as if he is alive (Harold says he had dinner with Paul in London). Jean discovers Bateman's journal drawings of murders and sexual violence. Bateman meets his lawyer and friends at lunch and tries to extract accountability, but his confession is dismissed as a joke or mistaken identity. Realizing his crimes go unpunished and may never be believed, Bateman concludes in voice-over that his confession "has meant nothing," leaving his violent double life unresolved.
